description: Modal 'na' presents a predicate as necessary. The source may be a physical constraint, logical conclusion, stated obligation, or practical need; context supplies the reading. The modal 'po' (POT) concerns possibility instead.
articulatory_notes: For dental 'n', the tongue tip rests against the backs of the upper teeth while voice passes through the nose. The contact releases as the tongue lowers into open-central 'a', and stress covers the resulting syllable.
usage_notes: Evidentiality precedes the modal rank, and negation follows it. 'Na ma' means must not, with the necessity applied to refraining. Absence of obligation uses the freedom expression 'thia lila wepu ralu nai' (you need not go), not a reordered particle stack.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Because 'na' makes necessity audible, a listener can ask what creates it. In an instruction, the particle records the necessity claim without turning it into consent.
